health.mental_health: Increased anxiety and irritability since the baby arrived; feeling stretched thin supporting partner through postpartum mood changes while managing his own sleep deprivation and health routines. Reports guilt about not being fully present at home or work and occasional panic-like episodes at night.
recent_visits: Primary care follow-up for elevated A1c and blood pressure medication adjustment (last month)
Sleep medicine telehealth check-in to review CPAP adherence and mask fit (6 weeks ago)
Diabetes nutrition counseling session (2 months ago)
Urgent care visit for persistent cough and reflux flare (3 months ago)

finance.debt: Car loan (~$14k remaining) and a growing balance on a credit card used for postpartum support expenses (night doula sessions, lactation consults, and baby gear). Still paying down a small medical bill from sleep clinic testing.
investments: HSA (used for diabetes and CPAP supplies)
recent_transactions: CPAP supplies and replacement mask purchase
Co-pays for primary care and diabetes counseling
Bulk formula and pharmacy runs for partner’s postpartum prescriptions
One-time payment for a night doula (two nights) to stabilize sleep at home
Increased childcare-related spending (diapers, stroller, baby monitor)

relationships: Partner: Maya Mensah (currently on maternity leave; experiencing postpartum mood swings and anxiety)
Close friend: Jordan Lee (checks in weekly; also a parent in Seattle)
Sister: Ewa Mensah (lives out of state; provides emotional support via calls)
Neighbor: Priya Nair (occasionally helps with grocery pickups)
social.social_media_activity: Low activity; occasionally posts baby photos to a private Instagram story and avoids public details about health and work. Uses a small parenting group on Facebook for tips but mostly lurks.
private_conversations: Discussing partner’s postpartum depression/anxiety symptoms and whether to seek additional treatment
Arguments about nighttime duties and resentment from sleep deprivation
Confiding concerns about his blood sugar control slipping since the baby arrived
Talking with a friend about fear of making a medication error at work due to exhaustion

schedule.work_hours: Typically Mon–Fri 8:00am–4:30pm, with occasional early starts for participant visits and periodic late documentation days; on-call phone coverage one weekend per month for urgent study issues.
regular_appointments: Weekly blood pressure and glucose check-ins (self-monitoring; logs reviewed monthly by PCP)
CPAP equipment cleaning/replacement routine (weekly maintenance; supplies reordered quarterly)
Biweekly couples check-in with a therapist (telehealth, evenings)
Monthly diabetes educator/nutrition follow-up (telehealth during lunch break)
upcoming_events: Pediatric 2-month well-baby visit and vaccinations (next week)
Sleep medicine follow-up to review CPAP data (in 3 weeks)
Quarterly study monitoring visit preparation deadline at work (end of month)
In-law visit to help with childcare for a long weekend (in 1 month)
schedule.daily_routine: Wakes around 5:30–6:00am depending on the baby’s night; checks fasting glucose, takes morning meds, commutes by light rail when possible. Workday is split between participant coordination, data entry, and regulatory tasks. Evenings revolve around feeding/soothing the baby, cleaning bottles, short walks when weather allows, and trying to get 5–6 hours of broken sleep with CPAP.

professional.company: University-affiliated medical center research unit in Seattle (e.g., UW Medicine–associated department)
projects: Phase II diabetes outcomes study coordinating participant visits and lab draws
Sleep apnea and cardiometabolic risk study (screening and follow-ups)
Regulatory binder maintenance and IRB continuing review submissions
professional.performance_issues: Struggling with documentation timeliness and occasional missed internal deadlines due to sleep deprivation and newborn-related emergencies. Worried about being perceived as unreliable; has requested flexible start times but hasn’t disclosed the full extent of home stress. Had one near-miss where he almost sent a de-identified dataset with an incorrect file name that could have risked re-identification if not caught.
workplace_relationships: Supportive PI who values his work but is demanding about audit readiness
Tense dynamic with a senior coordinator who comments on his recent lateness
Strong rapport with the clinic phlebotomy team who help accommodate last-minute participant reschedules
Frequent coordination with an external sponsor monitor who is strict about query turnaround times

hobbies: Weekend walks at Discovery Park (when childcare allows)
Cooking simple West African-inspired meals adapted for diabetes-friendly portions
Podcasts (science and true crime) during commutes
Occasional pickup soccer with coworkers (currently paused)
Photography on his phone (mostly family and Seattle waterfront)
habits: Uses CPAP nightly but sometimes removes it after waking with the baby
Tracks glucose and blood pressure in an app; occasionally skips logging on chaotic days
Drinks 2–3 coffees/day to compensate for poor sleep
Stress-eating late at night (chips/sweets) when anxious
Occasional cannabis edible on weekends to unwind (rare; worries about next-day grogginess)
lifestyle.living_situation: Rents a two-bedroom apartment in the Beacon Hill area of Seattle with his partner and newborn; limited space contributes to sleep disruptions (baby bassinet in their room). Considering a move closer to family support but constrained by Seattle rent prices.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Trying to follow a lower-carb, higher-fiber diet for diabetes (more vegetables, beans in measured portions, lean proteins). Struggles with consistency due to time and convenience foods during late-night feeds; aims to reduce sodium for hypertension.
